theme: Milling away the very material you clamp
title: Frame Mill: Clamp-Margin Sequencing
strategies: single-clamp reactive snap: whenever the next cut point drifts unsafe, yank the single farthest clamp to hug it, never planning ahead (cheap but geometrically blind)

row-major panic rebracket: whenever the cut point drifts unsafe, snap ALL four clamps into a tight bracket around just that one cell, re-approaching from the pad on every new row (safe locally, but the refixture bill explodes on long corridors)

column-major wavefront sweep: order removal by distance along the corridor, and before each batch of cuts pick a wide, forward-looking clamp bracket that already covers several batches ahead -- few repositions, consistently high margin
exact-optimal search (ILP/DP over the joint order+bracket space) -- intractable at scale, only useful to sanity check tiny instances
